title: PFMI ML Engineering Guide — Wheel Loader Predictive Maintenance

primary.note: Heavy class imbalance expected (~1–3% positive). Use SMOTE, class_weight, or focal loss.
Higher positive rate (~5–10%). Good for scheduling models.
Null values during healthy periods — use censoring-aware loss (Cox PH) or mask nulls.

primary.metric: Macro F1, Weighted F1, Confusion Matrix
primary.note: Severe class imbalance — 'No Failure' dominates. Sub-sample or use one-vs-rest.
Ordinal structure: Routine < Soon < Urgent. Consider ordinal regression.

recommended_train_test_split.strategy: Time-based split (never random shuffle for time-series)
recommended_train_test_split.note: Also consider per-machine hold-out to test generalization to unseen machines. Do NOT use random train/test split — future data leaks into training set.

LSTM_Transformer.rationale: Most failure precursors appear 6–24 hours before event. 288 steps captures full operational day.

short_context.rationale: Sufficient for fast-developing failures (Electrical, Fuel System).

long_context.rationale: Required for slow degradation trends (Bearing Wear, Transmission).

preprocessing_steps.name: Parse Timestamps
preprocessing_steps.detail: Convert ISO-8601 Timestamp to datetime; extract hour_of_day, day_of_week, month, season as cyclical sin/cos features.

preprocessing_steps.name: Sort by Machine_ID and Timestamp
preprocessing_steps.detail: Essential for correct time-series ordering before windowing.

preprocessing_steps.name: Handle Missing Values
preprocessing_steps.detail: Sensor missings (~0.3% per column): forward-fill within machine sessions (max 3 steps), then flag with binary missing indicator column (e.g., Coolant_Temperature_missing). Do NOT impute label columns.

preprocessing_steps.name: Detect and Cap Faulty Sensor Readings
preprocessing_steps.detail: Values outside 3× IQR per machine per column may be faulty sensors. Clip to machine-specific 1st–99th percentile. Add Faulty_Sensor_Flag binary column.

preprocessing_steps.name: Encode Categoricals
preprocessing_steps.detail: Manufacturer, Model: target-encode or embed. Terrain_Type, Weather, Working_Mode, Shift: ordinal or one-hot. Gear: map {1,2,3,4,N,R} → {1,2,3,4,0,-1}. Lubrication_Status: {Good:0, Fair:1, Poor:2}.

preprocessing_steps.name: Engineer Lag & Rolling Features
preprocessing_steps.detail: Per machine: rolling mean, std, min, max over {12, 72, 288} steps for: Overall_Vibration, Coolant_Temperature, Hydraulic_Pressure, Bearing_Temperature, Oil_Pressure, Turbo_Boost. Rate of change (first derivative) for same columns.

preprocessing_steps.name: Normalize / Scale
preprocessing_steps.detail: RobustScaler (median/IQR) preferred over StandardScaler due to outlier contamination from faulty sensors. Fit scaler on TRAIN set only; apply to val/test.

preprocessing_steps.name: Handle Class Imbalance
preprocessing_steps.detail: Failure_Within_24h: ~2% positive. Options: (a) class_weight='balanced'; (b) SMOTE on tabular features; (c) focal loss (gamma=2) for neural nets; (d) oversample failure windows.

preprocessing_steps.name: Create Sliding Windows for Sequence Models
preprocessing_steps.detail: Stride=1 for training data density. For production inference, stride=1 (predict every 5 minutes). Window must not cross maintenance events (maintenance resets degradation state).

preprocessing_steps.name: Censor RUL for Healthy Records
preprocessing_steps.detail: For survival models: records with Remaining_Useful_Life=null are right-censored observations (machine still healthy at observation time).

types.cause: CAN bus packet loss, telematics dropouts
Sensor hardware failure — column stuck at NaN for hours
Some older telematics units stop reporting during non-operational hours
types.affected: All columns, machines at specific job sites

actual_sensor_rates_onboard.Vibration_sensors: 1 ms (1 kHz) — RMS and FFT computed onboard, sent at 5-min

sensor_sampling_rates.note: High-frequency signals (vibration, FFT) are pre-processed on the edge device before transmission. Only summary statistics reach the cloud dataset.

anomaly_detection_features.why: Detects vibration spikes relative to machine-specific baseline — catches bearing defects

anomaly_detection_features.feature: Rate of change of Coolant_Temperature (delta per 12 steps)
anomaly_detection_features.why: Rapid temperature rise indicates cooling system failure onset
Divergence between pressure and efficiency indicates pump internal bypass

anomaly_detection_features.feature: Mahalanobis distance across [Coolant_Temp, Oil_Pressure, Vibration, Hyd_Pressure]
anomaly_detection_features.why: Multivariate anomaly capturing correlated sensor drift not visible in univariate checks

anomaly_detection_features.feature: Autoencoder reconstruction error on last 288-step window
anomaly_detection_features.why: Unsupervised baseline; high reconstruction error = operating outside training distribution

anomaly_detection_features.feature: Isolation Forest on rolling feature vector
anomaly_detection_features.why: Robust to mixed-type features; no label required for deployment
Ratio should be stable; decline indicates turbo efficiency loss

anomaly_detection_features.feature: Cross-tire pressure imbalance (max - min of 4 tire pressures)
anomaly_detection_features.why: Asymmetric loss indicates puncture or bead failure on specific tire
Ultrasonic is the earliest detectable signal for bearing and gear defects
Excess fuel consumption at given load indicates combustion inefficiency or injector drip

feature_importance_explanations.Overall_Vibration: Integrates all rotating machinery health into one signal — rises weeks before most mechanical failures.
feature_importance_explanations.Bearing_Vibration: Frequency-filtered signal isolating bearing fault frequencies — most sensitive early indicator for Bearing Wear.
feature_importance_explanations.Ultrasonic_Emission: Detects micro-cracks and micro-spalling at 40–100 kHz — leads visible damage by 2–6 weeks.
feature_importance_explanations.Coolant_Temperature: Rising coolant temp with stable load = cooling system degrading; crosses threshold hours before engine shutdown.
feature_importance_explanations.Oil_Pressure: Drops indicate pump wear or oil loss; low pressure is the mechanism of catastrophic engine bearing failure.
feature_importance_explanations.Hydraulic_Pressure: Directly tracks pump output; sustained drop = worn pump or internal bypass — precedes Hydraulic Pump Failure.
feature_importance_explanations.Pump_Efficiency: Computed ratio capturing slow pump degradation; efficiency drops before pressure drops.
feature_importance_explanations.Turbo_Boost: Boost decline at full load indicates compressor wheel erosion or seal bypass — Turbo Failure early warning.
feature_importance_explanations.Exhaust_Temperature: Rising EGT at constant load indicates combustion problems or DPF restriction.
feature_importance_explanations.Transmission_Temperature: Thermal stress of transmission fluid directly predicts clutch pack failure timelines.
feature_importance_explanations.Brake_Temperature: Brake temp spikes precede brake fade; pattern differs from normal braking at identical loads.
feature_importance_explanations.Engine_Hours: Primary machine life indicator — failure rate is exponential after 15,000 hours.
feature_importance_explanations.Oil_Age: Old oil loses viscosity index improvers; bearing wear accelerates sharply after 500 hours.
feature_importance_explanations.Last_Service_Days: Service interval adherence is the single most impactful maintenance variable for failure prevention.
feature_importance_explanations.Ambient_Temperature: Hot environments reduce cooling efficiency and accelerate rubber seal degradation across all systems.
feature_importance_explanations.Engine_Load: Load is the universal wear accelerant — all degradation rates scale with load fraction.
feature_importance_explanations.Dust_Level: Dust ingestion through worn seals is the #1 cause of hydraulic component failure in mining environments.
feature_importance_explanations.Operator_Experience: Experienced operators cause 30–50% fewer thermal stress events — a key confounding variable.
feature_importance_explanations.Tire_Pressure_FL/FR/RL/RR: Pressure loss triggers a thermal-mechanical cascade leading to blowout within hours.
feature_importance_explanations.Filter_Age: Old air filters starve engines; old hydraulic filters allow particulate contamination of pumps.
feature_importance_explanations.Previous_Failure_Count: Machines with 3+ prior failures are 2–3× more likely to fail again — structural or design-life issues.
feature_importance_explanations.FFT_Peak: Dominant spectral peak identifies specific fault mechanisms (e.g., 1× shaft imbalance, 8× gear mesh).
feature_importance_explanations.Fuel_Pressure: Rail pressure drop indicates pump wear or injector leakage — direct Fuel System Failure precursor.
feature_importance_explanations.Air_Filter_Restriction: High restriction reduces combustion air, raising EGT and reducing power before the engine ECU derate.
